      "suggestion": "Ensure the backend disable endpoint (apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/disable.ts) properly validates backup codes with rate limiting and timing attack protection, similar to TOTP validation."

      "description": "The EnableTwoFactorModal generates backup codes (setBackupCodes, setBackupCodesUrl) at SetupStep.DisplayBackupCodes, but the flow shows the user then proceeds to verify TOTP. If the backup codes are only stored in database after TOTP verification succeeds, and the user loses the backup codes before completing verification, there's a gap where backup codes exist but aren't accessible.",
      "suggestion": "Ensure backup codes are persisted to the database immediately when generated, before user proceeds to TOTP verification. Validate that backup codes are retrievable at all points in the enable flow."

      "description": "The BackupCode input field has minLength=10 and maxLength=11 to account for 'XXXXX-XXXXX' format, but there's no regex validation or format enforcement. A user could enter '12345-abcde' which passes length validation but may not match the intended format. The validation happens only on length, not on the actual backup code format.",
      "suggestion": "Add pattern validation to BackupCode field to enforce the expected format (e.g., /^[A-Z0-9]{5}-[A-Z0-9]{5}$/ or similar based on actual backup code generation logic)."
